games.title: Grand Theft Auto VI
games.description: Rockstar Games returns to Vice City in what is already the most anticipated game ever made. Set in modern-day Leonida (Rockstar's Florida), players follow Lucia and Jason in a Bonnie and Clyde-style criminal saga. The dual-protagonist system returns from GTA V with improvements — seamless switching during missions and dynamic interactions between the couple. Vice City has been recreated at an unprecedented scale with multiple distinct districts, beaches, swamps, and suburban sprawl. Dynamic world events, reactive NPCs, and systemic gameplay systems create emergent moments that rival scripted content. The attention to detail surpasses even Red Dead Redemption 2.

games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
games.description: Rockstar's open-world crime epic has transcended gaming to become a cultural institution, remaining one of the best-selling games over a decade after release. The story follows three protagonists — Michael, a retired bank robber in witness protection; Franklin, a repo man looking for a way out; and Trevor, a volatile psychopath — whose lives collide in Los Santos. The triple-protagonist system lets you switch between them instantly, experiencing the massive city of Los Santos from different perspectives. Heists are the centerpiece — planning, choosing crew members, and executing multi-stage robberies. GTA Online has grown into a standalone phenomenon with businesses, properties, races, and constant updates making it one of the most successful live-service games ever.

games.title: Red Dead Redemption 2
games.description: Rockstar Games delivers the most meticulously crafted open world ever created, set in the dying days of the American frontier in 1899. Players follow Arthur Morgan, a senior member of the Van der Linde gang, as they rob, hunt, and flee across five fictional U.S. states. The world responds to every action — greet strangers, help those in need, or terrorize townsfolk, and the game remembers. Hunting animals for food and pelts, maintaining your camp, cleaning your weapons, and managing your cores (health, stamina, dead eye) ground the experience in immersive realism. The story is a Shakespearean tragedy about loyalty, morality, and the end of an era.

games.title: Ghost of Tsushima
games.description: Sucker Punch Productions delivers a love letter to samurai cinema, casting players as Jin Sakai, the last samurai on Tsushima Island during the Mongol invasion of 1274. After a devastating defeat, Jin must abandon the honorable way of the samurai and become the Ghost — using stealth, poison, fear, and unconventional tactics to liberate his homeland. The game's guiding wind mechanic, haiku poetry, and bamboo-cutting mini-games infuse Japanese culture into every system. Combat is lethal and precise — standoffs let you eliminate enemies before they strike, while Ghost weapons create chaos in Mongol camps. Kurosawa Mode lets you play in black-and-white with film grain.

games.title: Ghost of Yotei
games.description: Sucker Punch returns to feudal Japan with a new story set 300 years after Ghost of Tsushima, in the shadow of Mount Yotei on Hokkaido. Players control Atsu, a warrior with a mysterious past, wielding new weapons including dual katanas, a chain-and-sickle, and a flintlock pistol. The world is the northern frontier — vast fields, snowy mountains, and frontier towns that feel distinct from Tsushima's settled south. Dynamic weather and seasons affect gameplay — blizzards reduce visibility, spring flowers reveal hidden paths, frozen lakes change traversal. The wind returns as your guide, but Atsu's story tackles themes of cultural erasure, colonization, and identity.

games.title: Resident Evil 4 Remake
games.description: Capcom rebuilds one of the most influential games ever made, preserving what made the original legendary while modernizing every system. Special agent Leon S. Kennedy is sent to rescue the President's daughter from a secluded Spanish village controlled by the cult Los Illuminados. The over-the-shoulder camera, originally revolutionary in 2005, returns with modern fluidity. The parry system — deflecting chainsaw attacks with a combat knife — adds new depth. The village is denser, Ganados are smarter, and the castle and island feel fresh while honoring the original. Separate Ways DLC adds Ada Wong's campaign with unique mechanics.

games.title: Resident Evil 2 Remake
games.description: Capcom's masterful reimagining of the 1998 classic preserves everything that made the original legendary while modernizing survival horror for a new generation. Leon Kennedy and Claire Redfield arrive in Raccoon City on the same night — separated by a car crash, they must navigate the zombie-infested city, the iconic Raccoon Police Station, and the underground Umbrella facility. Mr. X, the Tyrant, stalks you throughout the station — his footsteps echoing, his presence announced by the music change, creating constant tension. The A and B scenarios offer different perspectives and encounters. RE Engine delivers photorealistic gore and atmospheric lighting. Every corridor, every safe room, every licker encounter is precisely designed.

games.title: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard
games.description: Capcom saved survival horror by returning to the series' roots — first-person, limited resources, and a truly terrifying family of enemies. Ethan Winters searches for his missing wife in a derelict Louisiana plantation, only to discover the Baker family has been infected by a sentient mold. The game is claustrophobic and oppressive — the Baker house is a maze of rotting wood, hidden passages, and family dinner scenes from hell. Jack Baker stalks you with a shovel, Marguerite controls swarms of insects, and Lucas sets deadly traps. The VR mode (PSVR1) remains the most immersive horror experience ever made. Item management, puzzle solving, and that desperate scramble for ammo return Ethan to the genre's essence.
